  "findings": [
    "LEL, H2S, and CO atmospheric test readings are blank on the permit — required hazardous atmosphere checks not documented",
    "Meter bump-test record for today is not attached — instrument calibration/verification not confirmed before use",
    "Oxygen reading logged 30 minutes ago may exceed acceptable recency interval for pre-entry verification",
    "Permit is incomplete and does not meet minimum atmospheric testing documentation requirements"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Do not authorize entry on this permit",
    "Conduct full atmospheric testing: oxygen, LEL, H2S, and CO with a calibrated, bump-tested gas monitor",
    "Attach today's bump-test record to the permit before any entry",
    "Re-log all atmospheric readings on the permit at time of entry verification",
    "Verify readings are within acceptable ranges and within required time interval before authorizing entry"
  ],
  "rationale": "OSHA 1910.146 requires pre-entry atmospheric testing for oxygen content, flammable gases/vapors (LEL), and potential toxic contaminants before entry is authorized. The permit has only an oxygen reading while LEL, H2S, and CO fields are blank, meaning required atmospheric hazards have not been evaluated. The missing bump-test record means the instrument's accuracy cannot be verified. An incomplete permit with missing required atmospheric data cannot authorize entry.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1910.146(c)(5)(i) - evaluate hazardous atmospheres",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1910.146(d)(5) - test atmospheric conditions in permit space before entry",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1910.146(f) - permit content requirements including atmospheric testing results",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1910.146(d)(4)(i) - verify conditions are acceptable for entry"
  ]
}
